title: Controlling the sketch
text: far points displaced. 0 ruler-straight; default 1; values ~3 get loose scribbly.  bars:
code: x  <- seq(0, 10, length.out = 50) d  <- data.frame(x = x, y = sin(x))  ggplot(d, aes(x, y)) +   geom_sketch_line(aes(colour = "0.0  (straight)"), roughness = 0,   seed = 2) +   geom_sketch_line(aes(colour = "1.0  (default)"),  roughness = 1,   seed = 2) +   geom_sketch_line(aes(colour = "2.5"),             roughness = 2.5, seed = 2) +   geom_sketch_line(aes(colour = "5.0  (loose)"),    roughness = 5,   seed = 2) +   scale_colour_brewer("roughness", palette = "Set1") +   labs(title = "The roughness dial") +   theme_sketch() df <- data.frame(x = c("A", "B", "C"), y = c(4, 6, 3)) for (r in c(0, 1, 3)) {   print(     ggplot(df, aes(x, y)) +       geom_sketch_col(fill = "#7BAFD4", roughness = r, seed = 1L) +       labs(subtitle = paste("roughness =", r), x = NULL) +       theme_sketch(base_size = 9)   ) }

title: Controlling the sketch
text: much line segments bow (curve) endpoints. Higher values give springier, relaxed stroke.
code: seg <- data.frame(x = 1, y = 1, xend = 6, yend = 4) for (b in c(0, 2, 5)) {   print(     ggplot(seg) +       geom_sketch_segment(aes(x = x, y = y, xend = xend, yend = yend),                           bowing = b, roughness = 0.6, linewidth = 1,                           seed = 7L) +       labs(subtitle = paste("bowing =", b)) +       theme_sketch(base_size = 9)   ) }

title: Controlling the sketch
text: many overlaid strokes draw. 2 (default) gives classic “double-stroke” sketch look; 1 single pass; 3+ looks heavily worked.
code: for (n in c(1, 2, 4)) {   print(     ggplot(d, aes(x, y)) +       geom_sketch_line(n_passes = n, roughness = 1.5, linewidth = 0.7,                        colour = "grey20", seed = 3L) +       labs(subtitle = paste("n_passes =", n)) +       theme_sketch(base_size = 9)   ) }

title: Controlling the sketch
text: line family — geom_sketch_line(), geom_sketch_path(), geom_sketch_segment(), geom_sketch_step() — also takes medium, sets stroke laid : tapering pencil, wet ink line, crisp fountain pen, thin ballpoint, broad brush, charcoal, soft pastel, dusty chalk (best paper = "chalkboard"), marker, translucent highlighter band, crayon. sketch_media() lists .  can also map medium variable scale_medium_discrete() (see gallery). default, "pen", reproduces classic constant-width sketch line, existing plots unchanged.
code: for (m in c("pencil", "ink", "brush", "charcoal")) {   print(     ggplot(d, aes(x, y)) +       geom_sketch_line(medium = m, linewidth = 1.2, seed = 2L) +       labs(subtitle = paste("medium =", m)) +       theme_sketch(base_size = 9)   ) }

title: Controlling the sketch
text: sketch random, seeded. given seed always produces drawing, figures reproducible across renders devices. Change seed fresh “hand”.  first two panels identical (seed); third differs.
code: d2 <- data.frame(x = 1:8, y = c(2, 5, 3, 8, 6, 9, 7, 10)) for (s in c(1, 1, 42)) {   print(     ggplot(d2, aes(x, y)) +       geom_sketch_line(linewidth = 1, seed = s) +       geom_sketch_point(size = 3, seed = s + 100) +       labs(subtitle = paste("seed =", s)) +       theme_sketch(base_size = 9)   ) }

title: Controlling the sketch
text: pass seed, geoms use getOption("ggsketch.seed", 1L). Set make whole document reproducible: ggsketch never touches global random state: randomized routine draws seeded stream restores .Random.seed afterward, interfere set.seed() elsewhere analysis.
code: options(ggsketch.seed = 1L)

title: Fill styles in depth
code: styles <- c("hachure", "cross_hatch", "zigzag", "zigzag_line", "scribble",             "dots", "dashed", "stipple", "pencil_shade", "solid")  bars <- do.call(rbind, lapply(styles, function(s) {   data.frame(style = s, x = c("A", "B", "C"), y = c(4, 6, 3)) })) bars$style <- factor(bars$style, levels = styles)  # Draw a small panel per style by faceting and re-drawing each with its style. # (fill_style is a layer parameter, so we build one layer per facet.) plots <- lapply(styles, function(s) {   ggplot(subset(bars, style == s), aes(x, y)) +     geom_sketch_col(fill = "#E8A87C", fill_style = s, seed = 4L) +     labs(subtitle = s, x = NULL, y = NULL) +     theme_sketch(base_size = 9) })  # Show them individually: for (p in plots) print(p)

title: Fill styles in depth
text: fill_style = "watercolor" different beast: instead scan-line strokes lays several translucent, slightly bled scaled copies shape, plus fine granulation specks — painted wash rather drawn hatch. honours holes (works geom_sketch_contour_filled() bands donut slices) applies every polygon-, ribbon-, ellipse-, band-filled geom.  wash, hachure-tuning parameters (hachure_angle, hachure_gap, fill_weight) apply .
code: ang  <- seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = 9)[-9] hex  <- data.frame(x = cos(ang), y = sin(ang)) ggplot(hex, aes(x, y)) +   geom_sketch_polygon(fill = "#2E86C1", fill_style = "watercolor", seed = 1L) +   coord_equal() +   labs(title = "fill_style = \"watercolor\"") +   theme_sketch()

title: Fill styles in depth
text: Three parameters control texture.

title: Fill styles in depth
text: angle (degrees) fill lines:
code: df <- data.frame(x = c("A", "B", "C"), y = c(4, 6, 3)) for (a in c(0, 45, 90, 135)) {   print(     ggplot(df, aes(x, y)) +       geom_sketch_col(fill = "seagreen", hachure_angle = a, seed = 1L) +       labs(subtitle = paste("hachure_angle =", a), x = NULL) +       theme_sketch(base_size = 9)   ) }

title: Fill styles in depth
text: Spacing fill lines, data units — smaller means denser shading:
code: for (g in c(0.6, 1.2, 2.4)) {   print(     ggplot(df, aes(x, y)) +       geom_sketch_col(fill = "#5DADE2", hachure_gap = g, seed = 1L) +       labs(subtitle = paste("hachure_gap =", g), x = NULL) +       theme_sketch(base_size = 9)   ) }

title: Fill styles in depth
text: stroke weight fill lines:
code: for (w in c(0.3, 0.8, 1.6)) {   print(     ggplot(df, aes(x, y)) +       geom_sketch_col(fill = "#AF7AC5", fill_weight = w, seed = 1L) +       labs(subtitle = paste("fill_weight =", w), x = NULL) +       theme_sketch(base_size = 9)   ) }

title: Fill styles in depth
text: filler true scan-line algorithm, texture conforms polygon outline, just rectangles:
code: ang  <- seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = 9)[-9] hex  <- data.frame(x = cos(ang), y = sin(ang)) ggplot(hex, aes(x, y)) +   geom_sketch_polygon(fill = "#E74C3C", fill_style = "cross_hatch",                       hachure_angle = 30, seed = 1L) +   coord_equal() +   labs(title = "Cross-hatch on a hexagon") +   theme_sketch() td <- expand.grid(x = 1:5, y = 1:3) td$z <- td$x ggplot(td, aes(x, y, fill = z)) +   geom_sketch_tile(fill_style = "dots", seed = 1L) +   scale_fill_viridis_c() +   labs(title = "Dotted tiles") +   theme_sketch()

title: Fill styles in depth
text: fill geometry comes pure Layer-1 functions can call — useful testing custom grobs. sketch_fill() dispatches styles returns list line segments:
code: square_x <- c(0, 1, 1, 0) square_y <- c(0, 0, 1, 1) segs <- sketch_fill(square_x, square_y, fill_style = "hachure",                     hachure_gap = 0.15, seed = 1L) length(segs)      # number of fill segments #> [1] 8 head(segs[[1]])   # each segment is a matrix of x/y points #>              x          y #> [1,] 0.7093499 0.07633409 #> [2,] 0.8440814 0.20310311 #> [3,] 0.9270041 0.27937282

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_col() draws roughened outline hachure (pencil-shading) fill.  Map fill variable like ggplot2 bar. bar gets seed offset, two bars wobble identically.  geom_sketch_bar() counts rows (like geom_bar()):  Bars flip stack just like originals:
code: sales <- data.frame(product = c("Alpha", "Bravo", "Charlie", "Delta", "Echo"),                     units   = c(34, 51, 22, 47, 39))  ggplot(sales, aes(product, units)) +   geom_sketch_col(fill = "#7BAFD4", seed = 1L) +   labs(title = "Units sold", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch() ggplot(sales, aes(product, units, fill = product)) +   geom_sketch_col(seed = 2L, show.legend = FALSE) +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +   labs(title = "Mapped fill", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch() ggplot(mpg, aes(class)) +   geom_sketch_bar(fill = "#C39BD3", seed = 3L) +   labs(title = "Vehicle count by class", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch() +   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1)) ggplot(sales, aes(reorder(product, units), units)) +   geom_sketch_col(fill = "#F1948A", seed = 1L) +   coord_flip() +   labs(title = "Horizontal bars", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_chicklet() hand-drawn take ggchicklet::geom_chicklet(): stacked bar whose segments separately rounded “pills” small gap . Add coord_flip() classic horizontal layout.
code: seasons <- expand.grid(season = factor(2019:2024),                        result = c("Win", "Draw", "Loss")) seasons$games <- c(22, 18, 14, 20, 25, 19,                    8, 10, 12, 9, 6, 11,                    8, 10, 12, 9, 7, 8)  ggplot(seasons, aes(season, games, fill = result)) +   geom_sketch_chicklet(seed = 3L) +   coord_flip() +   scale_fill_manual(values = c(Win = "#4C9F70", Draw = "#E4B363",                                Loss = "#C1666B")) +   labs(title = "Season results by outcome", x = NULL, y = "Games",        fill = NULL) +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_lollipop() tidy alternative bars ranked sparse values: roughened stem baseline capped sketch point. value axis expands include baseline.
code: ggplot(sales, aes(reorder(product, units), units)) +   geom_sketch_lollipop(colour = "#7B241C", seed = 1L) +   labs(title = "Units sold", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_histogram() bins continuous variable draws hand-drawn bars; geom_sketch_freqpoly() draws counts roughened line.
code: ggplot(faithful, aes(eruptions)) +   geom_sketch_histogram(fill = "#7BAFD4", bins = 20, seed = 1L) +   labs(title = "Old Faithful eruption times") +   theme_sketch() ggplot(mpg, aes(hwy, colour = drv)) +   geom_sketch_freqpoly(bins = 15, linewidth = 0.9, seed = 2L) +   labs(title = "Highway mpg by drivetrain", x = "hwy") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_dotplot() bins data stacks one roughened circular dot per observation. dots sized bin width, count axis approximate — turn clean look.
code: ggplot(faithful, aes(eruptions)) +   geom_sketch_dotplot(binwidth = 0.12, fill = "#7BAFD4", seed = 1L) +   scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) +   labs(title = "Old Faithful eruption times") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_ecdf() draws empirical cumulative distribution hand-drawn stairstep.
code: ggplot(mpg, aes(hwy, colour = drv)) +   geom_sketch_ecdf(linewidth = 0.8, seed = 1L) +   labs(title = "Highway mpg ECDF by drivetrain", y = "F(x)") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_point() draws point small roughened ellipse:  Lines points compose like layers:  Multiple groups, one seed per group:
code: econ <- economics[economics$date > as.Date("2000-01-01"), ] ggplot(econ, aes(date, unemploy)) +   geom_sketch_line(colour = "steelblue", linewidth = 0.8, seed = 1L) +   labs(title = "US unemployment", x = NULL, y = "thousands") +   theme_sketch() ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, colour = factor(cyl))) +   geom_sketch_point(size = 4, seed = 1L) +   scale_colour_brewer("cylinders", palette = "Dark2") +   labs(title = "Fuel economy vs weight") +   theme_sketch() df <- data.frame(x = 1:12, y = c(3, 5, 4, 7, 6, 9, 8, 11, 9, 12, 11, 14)) ggplot(df, aes(x, y)) +   geom_sketch_line(colour = "grey40", seed = 3L) +   geom_sketch_point(size = 4, colour = "firebrick", seed = 8L) +   labs(title = "Trend with markers") +   theme_sketch() ggplot(ggplot2::economics_long, aes(date, value01, colour = variable)) +   geom_sketch_line(seed = 5L) +   labs(title = "Five series, hand-drawn", x = NULL, y = NULL) +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: size behaves like ggplot2 point size. Set constant bigger smaller markers:  map size variable bubble chart — pair scale_size_area() area (radius) encodes value:  small-multiples sweep single size aesthetic:
code: sz <- data.frame(x = 1:5, y = 1, s = c(2, 4, 6, 9, 13)) ggplot(sz, aes(x, y)) +   geom_sketch_point(size = sz$s, colour = "#2E86C1", seed = 1L) +   labs(title = "Fixed point sizes (2 → 13)", x = NULL, y = NULL) +   theme_sketch() ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, size = hp, colour = factor(cyl))) +   geom_sketch_point(alpha = 0.9, seed = 2L) +   scale_size_area("horsepower", max_size = 14) +   scale_colour_brewer("cylinders", palette = "Dark2") +   labs(title = "Bubble chart: size = horsepower") +   theme_sketch() grid <- expand.grid(x = 1:6, y = 1:3) grid$s <- seq(1.5, 11, length.out = nrow(grid)) ggplot(grid, aes(x, y, size = s)) +   geom_sketch_point(colour = "#884EA0", show.legend = FALSE, seed = 3L) +   scale_size_identity() +   labs(title = "Increasing point size", x = NULL, y = NULL) +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_point(), roughness mappable aesthetic. constant sets wobbly every marker — clean circles shaky:  Map variable values rescaled legible band scale_roughness_continuous() (applied automatically, default c(0.01, 0.75)), points can encode third variable shaky look:  Use () pass raw roughness unscaled, scale_roughness_continuous(range = ...) widen band.
code: rg <- data.frame(x = 1:4, y = 1, r = c(0, 0.4, 0.9, 1.6)) ggplot(rg, aes(x, y)) +   geom_sketch_point(aes(roughness = I(r)), size = 14, colour = "#2E86C1",                     seed = 1L) +   geom_sketch_text(aes(label = r), nudge_y = -0.5, size = 6) +   labs(title = "roughness 0 → 1.6 (constant per point)",        x = NULL, y = NULL) +   ylim(0.3, 1.3) +   theme_sketch() +   theme(axis.text = element_blank()) ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, roughness = hp, colour = factor(cyl))) +   geom_sketch_point(size = 4, seed = 1L) +   scale_colour_brewer("cylinders", palette = "Dark2") +   labs(title = "roughness mapped to horsepower") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_jitter() spreads overplotted points; geom_sketch_count() sizes single point many observations sit .
code: ggplot(mpg, aes(class, hwy)) +   geom_sketch_jitter(width = 0.2, height = 0, colour = "#5D6D7E",                      size = 2, seed = 1L) +   labs(title = "Jittered highway mpg", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch() +   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1)) ggplot(mpg, aes(cty, hwy)) +   geom_sketch_count(colour = "#C0392B", seed = 2L) +   scale_size_area(max_size = 8) +   labs(title = "Overplot count") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: sketchy heatmap geom_sketch_tile():  sketchy 2-D bin heatmap geom_sketch_bin2d() (cells default hachure fill, shaded count):  geom_sketch_hex() bins hexagons instead (needs hexbin package):
code: rects <- data.frame(xmin = c(1, 3, 5), xmax = c(2, 4, 6),                     ymin = 0, ymax = c(2, 4, 3)) ggplot(rects) +   geom_sketch_rect(aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax,                        fill = factor(xmin)),                    seed = 1L, show.legend = FALSE) +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_rect()") +   theme_sketch() td <- expand.grid(x = 1:8, y = 1:6) td$z <- td$x + td$y ggplot(td, aes(x, y, fill = z)) +   geom_sketch_tile(seed = 2L, hachure_gap = 0.18) +   scale_fill_viridis_c() +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_tile()") +   theme_sketch() ggplot(faithful, aes(eruptions, waiting)) +   geom_sketch_bin2d(bins = 12, seed = 3L) +   scale_fill_viridis_c() +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_bin2d()") +   theme_sketch() ggplot(faithful, aes(eruptions, waiting)) +   geom_sketch_hex(bins = 12, seed = 4L) +   scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "magma") +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_hex()") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: Concave polygons fill correctly (hachure respects every notch):
code: ang  <- seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = 11)[-11] r    <- rep(c(1, 0.45), length.out = 10) star <- data.frame(x = r * cos(ang), y = r * sin(ang)) ggplot(star, aes(x, y)) +   geom_sketch_polygon(fill = "tomato", seed = 1L) +   coord_equal() +   labs(title = "A concave star") +   theme_sketch() band <- data.frame(x = 1:20) band$y  <- 10 + 5 * sin(seq(0, 3 * pi, length.out = 20)) band$lo <- band$y - 2 band$hi <- band$y + 2  ggplot(band, aes(x)) +   geom_sketch_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), fill = "plum", seed = 2L) +   geom_sketch_line(aes(y = y), seed = 3L) +   labs(title = "Ribbon + line") +   theme_sketch() ggplot(band, aes(x, y)) +   geom_sketch_area(fill = "lightgreen", seed = 3L) +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_area()") +   theme_sketch() ggplot(faithful, aes(eruptions)) +   geom_sketch_density(fill = "khaki", seed = 4L) +   labs(title = "Old Faithful eruptions") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_violin() mirrors kernel density closed polygon hachure-fills .
code: ggplot(mpg, aes(class, hwy, fill = class)) +   geom_sketch_violin(seed = 1L, show.legend = FALSE) +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set3") +   labs(title = "Highway mpg distribution by class", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch() +   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))

title: Gallery — every geom
text: hand-drawn fit roughened confidence band:
code: ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_sketch_point(seed = 1L) +   geom_sketch_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, seed = 2L) +   labs(title = "Linear fit with CI band") +   theme_sketch() ggplot(mpg, aes(displ, hwy)) +   geom_sketch_point(colour = "grey50", seed = 1L) +   geom_sketch_smooth(seed = 2L, colour = "darkorange") +   labs(title = "loess fit") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_function() sketches analytic curve x range, example overlay theoretical density.
code: ggplot(data.frame(x = c(-4, 4)), aes(x)) +   geom_sketch_function(fun = dnorm, colour = "#2E86C1", linewidth = 0.9,                        seed = 1L) +   geom_sketch_function(fun = dnorm, args = list(sd = 1.6),                        colour = "#C0392B", linewidth = 0.9, seed = 2L) +   labs(title = "Two normal densities", y = "density") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_qq() draws quantile-quantile points geom_sketch_qq_line() reference line. Map data sample aesthetic.
code: ggplot(mtcars, aes(sample = mpg)) +   geom_sketch_qq(size = 2.5, seed = 1L) +   geom_sketch_qq_line(colour = "#C8553D", linewidth = 0.8, seed = 2L) +   labs(title = "Normal Q-Q plot of mpg", x = "theoretical", y = "sample") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_quantile() fits draws quantile regression lines (requires optional quantreg package).
code: ggplot(mpg, aes(displ, hwy)) +   geom_sketch_point(colour = "grey60", seed = 1L) +   geom_sketch_quantile(quantiles = c(0.1, 0.5, 0.9), colour = "#6C3483",                        linewidth = 0.9, seed = 2L) +   labs(title = "10th / 50th / 90th percentile fits") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: Radii data units, use coord_equal() true circles:
code: cdf <- data.frame(x = c(1, 3, 2), y = c(1, 1, 2.5),                   r = c(0.6, 0.9, 0.5), grp = c("a", "b", "c")) ggplot(cdf, aes(x, y, r = r, fill = grp)) +   geom_sketch_circle(seed = 1L, show.legend = FALSE) +   coord_equal() +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_circle()") +   theme_sketch() edf <- data.frame(x = c(1, 3), y = c(1, 2), a = c(1.4, 0.8), b = c(0.6, 1.2)) ggplot(edf, aes(x, y, a = a, b = b, fill = factor(x))) +   geom_sketch_ellipse(seed = 2L, show.legend = FALSE) +   coord_equal() +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_ellipse()") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
code: sdf <- data.frame(x = 1:4, y = c(1, 3, 2, 4),                   xend = 2:5, yend = c(3, 1, 4, 2)) ggplot(sdf) +   geom_sketch_segment(aes(x = x, y = y, xend = xend, yend = yend),                       colour = "darkgreen", linewidth = 1, seed = 3L) +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_segment()") +   theme_sketch() stp <- data.frame(x = 1:8, y = c(1, 3, 2, 5, 4, 6, 5, 8)) ggplot(stp, aes(x, y)) +   geom_sketch_step(colour = "purple", linewidth = 1, seed = 4L) +   geom_sketch_point(seed = 5L) +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_step()") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_curve() curved connector (quadratic Bézier); curvature sets much bends.  geom_sketch_spoke() draws segment point angle radius — useful vector fields.
code: cdf <- data.frame(x = c(1, 1, 1), y = c(1, 2, 3),                   xend = c(4, 4, 4), yend = c(1, 2, 3)) ggplot(cdf, aes(x, y)) +   geom_sketch_curve(aes(xend = xend, yend = yend), curvature = 0.4,                     colour = "#1A5276", linewidth = 0.9, seed = 1L) +   geom_sketch_point(seed = 2L) +   geom_sketch_point(aes(x = xend, y = yend), seed = 3L) +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_curve()") +   theme_sketch() field <- expand.grid(x = 1:6, y = 1:6) field$angle  <- with(field, atan2(y - 3.5, x - 3.5)) field$radius <- 0.6 ggplot(field, aes(x, y)) +   geom_sketch_spoke(aes(angle = angle, radius = radius),                     colour = "#117A65", seed = 1L) +   geom_sketch_point(size = 1.5, seed = 2L) +   coord_equal() +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_spoke()") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_rug() adds marginal ticks along panel edges (sides).
code: ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_sketch_point(colour = "#2C3E50", seed = 1L) +   geom_sketch_rug(sides = "bl", colour = "#7B241C", seed = 2L) +   labs(title = "Scatter with marginal rug") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: interval family draws hand-drawn ranges: geom_sketch_linerange(), geom_sketch_pointrange(), geom_sketch_errorbar(), geom_sketch_crossbar().
code: est <- data.frame(   group = c("A", "B", "C", "D"),   mean  = c(4.1, 5.6, 3.2, 6.0),   lo    = c(3.2, 4.9, 2.4, 5.1),   hi    = c(5.0, 6.4, 4.1, 6.8) ) ggplot(est, aes(group, mean)) +   geom_sketch_pointrange(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), colour = "#1F618D",                          seed = 1L) +   labs(title = "Point estimates with 95% intervals", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch() ggplot(est, aes(group, mean)) +   geom_sketch_col(fill = "#AED6F1", width = 0.6, seed = 1L) +   geom_sketch_errorbar(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), width = 0.3, seed = 2L) +   labs(title = "Bars with error bars", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch() ggplot(est, aes(group, mean)) +   geom_sketch_crossbar(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), fill = "#FCF3CF",                        fill_style = "hachure", seed = 3L) +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_crossbar()", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_abline(), geom_sketch_hline(), geom_sketch_vline() span panel gentle wobble.
code: ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_sketch_point(seed = 1L) +   geom_sketch_hline(yintercept = 20, colour = "#C0392B", seed = 2L) +   geom_sketch_vline(xintercept = 3.3, colour = "#2471A3", seed = 3L) +   geom_sketch_abline(slope = -5, intercept = 37, colour = "#117864",                      linetype = 2, seed = 4L) +   labs(title = "Reference lines") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_contour() draws contour lines surface (needs z); geom_sketch_density2d() contours 2-D kernel density estimate.   geom_sketch_contour_filled() fills bands levels instead just lines. band may contain holes (next level , cut ); hole-aware filler keeps empty, fill_style = "hachure" works .
code: ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, z = density)) +   geom_sketch_contour(colour = "#2E4053", seed = 1L) +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_contour()") +   theme_sketch() ggplot(faithful, aes(eruptions, waiting)) +   geom_sketch_point(colour = "grey70", seed = 1L) +   geom_sketch_density2d(colour = "#884EA0", linewidth = 0.7, seed = 2L) +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_density2d()") +   theme_sketch() ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, z = density)) +   geom_sketch_contour_filled(seed = 1L) +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_contour_filled()") +   theme_sketch() ggplot(faithful, aes(eruptions, waiting)) +   geom_sketch_density_2d_filled(fill_style = "hachure", seed = 2L) +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_density_2d_filled() (hachure)") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_engrave() shades surface way etcher banknote engraver : continuous tone built density hatch lines, cross-hatching deepening shadows. takes x/y/z grid; high z dark. Unlike fill-pattern packages computes tone geometry rather tiling motif.  pre-computed grid, shade raw points density stat:  geom_sketch_shade() shades polygon region uniform density set tone aesthetic [0, 1], mapped value reads directly darkness. Mapping raw variable tone rescales scale_tone_continuous() (alias scale_engrave()), just scale_size() rescales size.
code: ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, z = density)) +   geom_sketch_engrave(seed = 1L) +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_engrave()") +   theme_sketch() ggplot(faithful, aes(eruptions, waiting)) +   geom_sketch_engrave(stat = "density_2d", contour = FALSE,                       aes(z = after_stat(density)), seed = 1L) +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_engrave() from raw points") +   theme_sketch() hex <- data.frame(   x = cos(seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = 7))[-7],   y = sin(seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = 7))[-7] ) regions <- do.call(rbind, lapply(1:3, function(k) {   transform(hex, x = x + (k - 1) * 2.3, g = k, val = c(0.25, 0.55, 0.9)[k]) })) ggplot(regions, aes(x, y, group = g)) +   geom_sketch_shade(aes(tone = val), seed = 2L) +   coord_equal() +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_shade(): value → density") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: sketch text handwriting font, roughened glyphs. geom_sketch_text() uses first installed handwriting face (falls back device default otherwise).
code: lab <- data.frame(x = c(2, 4, 3), y = c(3, 4, 1.5),                   txt = c("hand", "drawn", "labels")) ggplot(lab, aes(x, y, label = txt)) +   geom_sketch_point(size = 3, colour = "#C0392B") +   geom_sketch_text(size = 7, nudge_y = 0.4) +   scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = 0.15)) +   scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = 0.12)) +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_text()") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: composed geom: rough IQR box, thick median, whiskers, sketchy outliers.  default box outline-(fill NA). Give fill solid box, map fill switch fill style coloured, shaded boxes:
code: ggplot(mpg, aes(class, hwy)) +   geom_sketch_boxplot(seed = 1L) +   labs(title = "Highway mpg by class", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch() +   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1)) ggplot(mpg, aes(class, hwy, fill = class)) +   geom_sketch_boxplot(fill_style = "hachure", seed = 1L, show.legend = FALSE) +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Pastel1") +   labs(title = "Hachure-filled boxes", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch() +   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))

title: Gallery — every geom
text: annotate_sketch() adds one-hand-drawn marks (aes() inheritance):
code: ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_sketch_point(seed = 1L) +   annotate_sketch("rect", xmin = 3, xmax = 4, ymin = 15, ymax = 22,                   fill = NA, colour = "red", seed = 2L) +   annotate_sketch("segment", x = 2, y = 32, xend = 3.4, yend = 21,                   colour = "blue", linewidth = 1, seed = 3L) +   annotate_sketch("circle", x = 5, y = 30, r = 0.4,                   colour = "darkgreen", fill = "green", seed = 4L) +   labs(title = "Highlighting with annotate_sketch()") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_bracket() draws hand-drawn comparison bracket optional handwriting label, marking pairwise comparisons (sketchy ggsignif).
code: brackets <- data.frame(   xmin  = c(1, 2),   xmax  = c(2, 3),   y     = c(40, 45),   label = c("p = 0.03", "n.s.") ) ggplot(mpg, aes(drv, hwy)) +   geom_sketch_boxplot(seed = 1L) +   geom_sketch_bracket(     data = brackets,     aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, y = y, label = label),     seed = 2L   ) +   labs(title = "Pairwise comparisons", x = "drivetrain") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_pie() draws hand-drawn pie sized amount aesthetic coloured fill. Slices stay circular panel shape, look right without coord_fixed(); pair theme_void() drop unused axes.  geom_sketch_donut() hole; fill_style hatches slices instead filling solid.
code: shares <- data.frame(   group  = c("Sketch", "Polish", "Coffee", "Doubt"),   amount = c(40, 25, 20, 15) )  ggplot(shares, aes(amount = amount, fill = group)) +   geom_sketch_pie(seed = 1L) +   scale_fill_sketch() +   labs(title = "Where the time goes") +   coord_fixed() +   theme_void() ggplot(shares, aes(amount = amount, fill = group)) +   geom_sketch_donut(fill_style = "hachure", seed = 2L) +   scale_fill_sketch() +   theme_void()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: Rectangular geoms (geom_sketch_rect(), geom_sketch_tile(), geom_sketch_col()/bar()) take corner_radius rounded corners — fraction half-side, 0 square 1 fully rounded.
code: ggplot(shares, aes(group, amount, fill = group)) +   geom_sketch_col(corner_radius = 0.25, fill_style = "solid", seed = 1L,                   show.legend = FALSE) +   scale_fill_sketch() +   labs(title = "Rounded columns", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: annotate_sketch_arrow() points feature hand-drawn arrow. content-aware: shaft curves automatically toward target, arrowhead orients curve’s end tangent, label sits clear shaft. number curvature (0 straight) overrides automatic bow, arrow_type = "closed" gives filled head.
code: ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_sketch_point(colour = "grey30", seed = 1L) +   annotate_sketch_arrow(x = 4.1, y = 33, xend = 5.25, yend = 18,                         label = "heavy & thirsty", colour = "#C0392B",                         seed = 2L) +   annotate_sketch_arrow(x = 2.2, y = 12, xend = 1.7, yend = 30,                         label = "light & frugal", colour = "#1F618D",                         arrow_type = "closed", seed = 3L) +   labs(title = "Pointing things out") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: annotate_sketch_callout() puts handwriting note roughened rounded box auto-sizes text, leader arrow target. Omit xend/yend plain boxed label.
code: ggplot(faithful, aes(eruptions, waiting)) +   geom_sketch_point(colour = "grey40", seed = 1L) +   annotate_sketch_callout(x = 2.1, y = 95, label = "short bursts",                           xend = 1.9, yend = 75, fill = "#EAF2F8",                           colour = "#1F618D", seed = 2L) +   labs(title = "Boxed callouts") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom

code: ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Sepal.Width, colour = Species)) +   geom_sketch_mark_hull(aes(fill = Species), expand = 0.08, seed = 1L) +   geom_sketch_point(seed = 2L) +   scale_colour_sketch() +   scale_fill_sketch() +   labs(title = "Grouping clusters") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_mark_ellipse(), geom_sketch_mark_circle(), geom_sketch_mark_rect() complete family — roughened ellipse, circle, rectangle around group. panel expands fit mark, never clipped.
code: ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Sepal.Width, colour = Species)) +   geom_sketch_mark_ellipse(aes(fill = Species), seed = 1L) +   geom_sketch_point(seed = 2L) +   scale_colour_sketch() +   scale_fill_sketch() +   labs(title = "Bounding ellipses") +   theme_sketch() ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Sepal.Width, colour = Species)) +   geom_sketch_mark_rect(seed = 3L) +   geom_sketch_point(seed = 2L) +   scale_colour_sketch() +   labs(title = "Bounding rectangles") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: ggsketch 2.0 grows two directions: new chart families (built roughened grobs, inherit every fill style free) drawing-medium simulator — strokes imitate pencil, ink, brush, charcoal, marker crayon, watercolour washes, textured papers, hand-drawn coords.

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_dumbbell() draws connector two values per row, capped sketch dot end — ideal /comparisons.  geom_sketch_slope() connects group across two () categories, showing ranking changes.
code: dumb <- data.frame(g = c("Alpha", "Bravo", "Charlie", "Delta"),                    before = c(20, 35, 28, 42),                    after  = c(34, 51, 22, 47)) ggplot(dumb, aes(x = before, xend = after, y = reorder(g, after))) +   geom_sketch_dumbbell(colour_x = "#B03A2E", colour_xend = "#1F618D",                        seed = 1L) +   labs(title = "Before vs after", x = "value", y = NULL) +   theme_sketch() slope <- data.frame(   time  = factor(rep(c("Before", "After"), each = 4),                  levels = c("Before", "After")),   value = c(20, 35, 28, 42, 34, 51, 22, 47),   who   = rep(c("Alpha", "Bravo", "Charlie", "Delta"), 2) ) ggplot(slope, aes(time, value, group = who, colour = who)) +   geom_sketch_slope(seed = 1L) +   scale_colour_sketch() +   labs(title = "Slope chart", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_waterfall() floats step’s delta running total running total , dotted hand-drawn connectors carrying level across gaps. Rows flagged measure = "total" draw running total zero.
code: ledger <- data.frame(   step  = factor(c("Start", "Sales", "Refunds", "Costs", "Tax", "Net"),                  levels = c("Start", "Sales", "Refunds", "Costs", "Tax", "Net")),   delta = c(120, 80, -25, -60, -18, 0),   kind  = c("relative", "relative", "relative", "relative", "relative", "total") ) ggplot(ledger, aes(step, delta, measure = kind)) +   geom_sketch_waterfall(seed = 1L) +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_waterfall()", x = NULL, y = "amount") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_funnel() centres one bar per stage zero, width stage’s value, translucent trapezoids carrying stage next. geom_sketch_pyramid() mirrors bars zero two-level side aesthetic — population pyramid.
code: funnel <- data.frame(   stage = factor(c("Visited", "Signed up", "Activated", "Paid"),                  levels = rev(c("Visited", "Signed up", "Activated", "Paid"))),   n     = c(1200, 460, 210, 80) ) ggplot(funnel, aes(n, stage, fill = stage)) +   geom_sketch_funnel(seed = 1L, show.legend = FALSE) +   scale_fill_sketch() +   scale_x_continuous(labels = abs) +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_funnel()", x = "users", y = NULL) +   theme_sketch() pop <- data.frame(   age = factor(rep(c("0-19", "20-39", "40-59", "60+"), 2),                levels = c("0-19", "20-39", "40-59", "60+")),   sex = rep(c("Female", "Male"), each = 4),   n   = c(340, 420, 380, 240, 360, 440, 370, 200) ) ggplot(pop, aes(n, age, side = sex, fill = sex)) +   geom_sketch_pyramid(seed = 1L) +   scale_fill_manual(values = c(Female = "#b56b6f", Male = "#5b7290")) +   scale_x_continuous(labels = abs) +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_pyramid()", x = "count", y = NULL) +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_beeswarm() spreads points sideways none overlap, keeping exact value — deterministic, seeded swarm.
code: ggplot(iris, aes(Species, Sepal.Length, colour = Species)) +   geom_sketch_beeswarm(size = 2.5, seed = 1L, show.legend = FALSE) +   scale_colour_sketch() +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_beeswarm()", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_ridgeline() stacks per-group densities overlapping ridges, drawn back--front. fill_style works, including watercolour.
code: ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Species, fill = Species)) +   geom_sketch_ridgeline(scale = 1.6, seed = 1L, show.legend = FALSE) +   scale_fill_sketch() +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_ridgeline()", y = NULL) +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_streamgraph() stacks group values around flowing baseline (offset = "silhouette", "zero", "wiggle").
code: set.seed(1) stream <- expand.grid(t = 1:12, grp = c("a", "b", "c", "d")) stream$v <- abs(sin(stream$t / 3 + match(stream$grp, letters)) + 1.2) * 5 ggplot(stream, aes(t, v, fill = grp)) +   geom_sketch_streamgraph(seed = 1L) +   scale_fill_sketch() +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_streamgraph()", x = "time", y = NULL) +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_waffle() turns counts grid squares (largest-remainder rounding 100 cells default).  geom_sketch_treemap() lays nested rectangles area (squarified treemap) can label tile.
code: waf <- data.frame(grp = c("Rent", "Food", "Travel", "Other"),                   spend = c(45, 25, 20, 10)) ggplot(waf, aes(fill = grp, weight = spend)) +   geom_sketch_waffle(seed = 1L) +   scale_fill_sketch() +   coord_equal() +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_waffle()") +   theme_void() tm <- data.frame(grp = c("Alpha", "Bravo", "Charlie", "Delta", "Echo"),                  val = c(40, 25, 15, 12, 8)) ggplot(tm, aes(area = val, fill = grp, label = grp)) +   geom_sketch_treemap(seed = 1L, show.legend = FALSE) +   scale_fill_sketch() +   coord_equal() +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_treemap()") +   theme_void()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_calendar() maps date aesthetic onto GitHub-style grid weeks (columns) weekdays (rows).
code: set.seed(1) cal <- data.frame(day = as.Date("2024-01-01") + 0:180) cal$value <- cumsum(rnorm(nrow(cal))) ggplot(cal, aes(date = day, fill = value)) +   geom_sketch_calendar(seed = 1L) +   scale_fill_viridis_c() +   coord_equal() +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_calendar()") +   theme_sketch() +   theme(axis.title = element_blank())

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_gantt() draws one bar per task x (start) xend (end) discrete y — whiteboard project plan. Map progress (0–1) overlay completed fraction darker solid bar.
code: plan <- data.frame(   task  = factor(c("Design", "Build", "Test", "Ship"),                  levels = rev(c("Design", "Build", "Test", "Ship"))),   start = as.Date(c("2026-01-05", "2026-01-19", "2026-02-09", "2026-02-23")),   end   = as.Date(c("2026-01-23", "2026-02-13", "2026-02-27", "2026-03-06")),   done  = c(1, 0.7, 0.25, 0) ) ggplot(plan, aes(start, xend = end, y = task, fill = task, progress = done)) +   geom_sketch_gantt(seed = 1L, show.legend = FALSE) +   scale_fill_sketch() +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_gantt()", x = NULL, y = NULL) +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: sketch_graph() turns edge list (igraph object) ready--plot nodes edges frames, placing nodes pure-R force-directed layout — feature needs graph dependency. Draw result geom_sketch_edge() (roughened, optionally curved connectors) geom_sketch_node() (roughened markers optional handwriting labels).  node edge column carried sketch_graph() maps like normal aesthetic, curvature bows edges. node size encodes degree:  Pass igraph object straight sketch_graph() richer generators graph algorithms:
code: edges <- data.frame(   from = c("A", "A", "A", "B", "C", "C", "D", "E", "E", "F", "B", "G"),   to   = c("B", "C", "D", "C", "D", "E", "E", "F", "G", "G", "E", "H") ) g <- sketch_graph(edges, seed = 1L)  ggplot() +   geom_sketch_edge(data = g$edges,                    aes(x = x, y = y, xend = xend, yend = yend),                    colour = "grey55", seed = 1L) +   geom_sketch_node(data = g$nodes, aes(x = x, y = y, label = name),                    size = 7, colour = "#1F618D", seed = 2L) +   coord_equal() +   labs(title = "A hand-drawn network") +   theme_void() deg <- table(c(edges$from, edges$to)) g2  <- sketch_graph(edges, seed = 4L) g2$nodes$degree <- as.integer(deg[g2$nodes$name])  ggplot() +   geom_sketch_edge(data = g2$edges,                    aes(x = x, y = y, xend = xend, yend = yend),                    curvature = 0.25, colour = "#B9770E", seed = 3L) +   geom_sketch_node(data = g2$nodes,                    aes(x = x, y = y, size = degree, label = name),                    colour = "#7D3C98", show.legend = FALSE, seed = 4L) +   scale_size_area(max_size = 13) +   coord_equal() +   labs(title = "Curved edges, nodes sized by degree") +   theme_void() set.seed(1) ig <- igraph::sample_pa(16, directed = FALSE) g3 <- sketch_graph(ig, seed = 7L)  ggplot() +   geom_sketch_edge(data = g3$edges,                    aes(x = x, y = y, xend = xend, yend = yend),                    colour = "grey60", seed = 5L) +   geom_sketch_node(data = g3$nodes, aes(x = x, y = y),                    size = 5, colour = "#148F77", seed = 6L) +   coord_equal() +   labs(title = "A preferential-attachment graph (via igraph)") +   theme_void()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_sf() sketch take geom_sf(): one call roughens whichever simple-features geometry present — (MULTI)POLYGON features get hole-aware hachure (fill_style), (MULTI)LINESTRING features become sketch paths, (MULTI)POINT features become sketch points. needs optional sf package plots planar coordinates (pre-project lon/lat data sf::st_transform() faithful map).  Switch fill_style, exactly like sketch fill:
code: nc <- sf::st_read(system.file("shape/nc.shp", package = "sf"), quiet = TRUE)  ggplot() +   geom_sketch_sf(data = nc, aes(fill = BIR74), seed = 1L) +   scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "magma") +   labs(title = "North Carolina births, 1974") +   theme_void() ggplot() +   geom_sketch_sf(data = nc, aes(fill = SID74), fill_style = "cross_hatch",                  seed = 2L) +   scale_fill_distiller(palette = "RdPu", direction = 1) +   labs(title = "Cross-hatched choropleth of SIDS cases") +   theme_void()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_radar() draws spider chart: series closed polygon evenly spaced axes, roughened web (rings, spokes, labels) behind. Map axis, value, group, colour/fill; like pie geoms lives square space, pair coord_equal() theme_void().  single series reads cleanly watercolour wash grid rings:
code: skills <- data.frame(   axis  = rep(c("Speed", "Power", "Range", "Control", "Stamina", "Magic"), 2),   value = c(8, 6, 9, 5, 7, 4, 5, 9, 4, 8, 6, 9),   who   = rep(c("Aria", "Bilo"), each = 6) ) ggplot(skills, aes(axis = axis, value = value, group = who,                    colour = who, fill = who)) +   geom_sketch_radar(alpha = 0.3, seed = 1L) +   scale_colour_sketch() +   scale_fill_sketch() +   coord_equal() +   labs(title = "Character stats") +   theme_void() ggplot(subset(skills, who == "Aria"),        aes(axis = axis, value = value, group = who)) +   geom_sketch_radar(fill = "#2E86C1", fill_style = "watercolor",                     n_rings = 5, seed = 3L) +   coord_equal() +   labs(title = "One series, watercolour") +   theme_void()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_chord() lays nodes circle — given rim arc sized total flow — draws every weighted relation ribbon centre. Give edge table , , value columns; ribbons colour source node.
code: trade <- data.frame(   from  = c("Asia", "Asia", "Europe", "Africa", "Africa", "America",             "America", "Asia"),   to    = c("Europe", "America", "America", "Asia", "Europe", "Africa",             "Europe", "Africa"),   value = c(8, 6, 5, 3, 2, 4, 7, 5) ) ggplot() +   geom_sketch_chord(trade, from, to, value, seed = 1L) +   scale_fill_sketch() +   coord_equal() +   labs(title = "Trade flows") +   theme_void()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_bump() draws series’ rank every time point, joined across adjacent times smooth roughened curves crossing reads overtake. Give long data time, series, value columns.
code: standings <- data.frame(   year = rep(2018:2023, each = 5),   team = rep(c("Falcons", "Bears", "Wolves", "Hawks", "Lions"), times = 6),   pts  = c(20, 18, 15, 12, 10,  15, 20, 18, 10, 12,  18, 12, 20, 15, 8,            12, 18, 15, 20, 14,  10, 15, 12, 18, 22,  22, 14, 10, 16, 18) ) ggplot() +   geom_sketch_bump(standings, year, team, pts, seed = 4L, point_size = 4) +   scale_colour_sketch() +   labs(title = "Season standings") +   theme_void() +   theme(legend.position = "none", plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_alluvial() draws two categorical axes stacks strata, joined flows whose thickness frequency category combination. Give wide data frame, axes columns order, optional value weight; flows colour first axis (named fill column).
code: titanic <- as.data.frame(Titanic) ggplot() +   geom_sketch_alluvial(titanic, axes = c("Class", "Sex", "Age", "Survived"),                        value = "Freq", seed = 1L) +   scale_fill_sketch() +   labs(title = "Titanic passengers", fill = "Class") +   theme_void()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_parallel() draws several numeric columns vertical axes every observation roughened polyline crossing . Axes scale independently; map colour grouping column.
code: ggplot() +   geom_sketch_parallel(iris,     axes = c("Sepal.Length", "Sepal.Width", "Petal.Length", "Petal.Width"),     colour = "Species", alpha = 0.6, seed = 1L) +   scale_colour_sketch() +   labs(title = "Iris parallel coordinates") +   theme_void() +   theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_mosaic() splits square columns marginal counts x, column conditional counts y, every tile’s area joint frequency.
code: titanic <- as.data.frame(Titanic) ggplot() +   geom_sketch_mosaic(titanic, x = Class, y = Survived, value = Freq,                      seed = 1L) +   scale_fill_sketch() +   labs(title = "Survival by class", fill = "Survived") +   theme_void() +   theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_rose() gives category equal angular wedge whose radius (, area_true = TRUE, whose area) encodes value — Florence Nightingale’s coxcomb. optional fill stacks radially within wedge.
code: mortality <- data.frame(   quarter = rep(c("Q1", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4", "Q5", "Q6"), each = 3),   cause   = rep(c("Disease", "Wounds", "Other"), times = 6),   n       = c(20, 8, 4,  30, 10, 5,  18, 6, 3,  12, 9, 2,  8, 5, 2,  14, 7, 3) ) ggplot() +   geom_sketch_rose(mortality, quarter, n, fill = cause, area_true = TRUE,                    seed = 8L, alpha = 0.85) +   scale_fill_sketch() +   labs(title = "Mortality by quarter (area-true)", fill = NULL) +   coord_equal() +   theme_void() +   theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_marimekko() draws variable-width stacked bars: column width one category’s share total, stacked segments second category’s shares, tile’s area joint value. Width percentages sit top.
code: revenue_mix <- data.frame(   region  = rep(c("North America", "Europe", "Asia", "Other"), each = 3),   product = rep(c("Phones", "Laptops", "Tablets"), times = 4),   revenue = c(50, 35, 15,  30, 40, 20,  45, 25, 30,  10, 12, 6) ) ggplot() +   geom_sketch_marimekko(revenue_mix, region, product, revenue, seed = 6L) +   scale_fill_sketch() +   labs(title = "Revenue share by region and product", fill = NULL) +   theme_void() +   theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),         plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 18, 10))

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_sunburst() draws hierarchy nested rings annular sectors: columns levels go inner root ring outward, deeper ring splits parent’s angle children’s summed value, child always nests inside parent. Fill top-level ancestor classic look.
code: gss <- data.frame(   region = rep(c("West", "East", "North"), each = 4),   dept   = rep(c("Sales", "Sales", "Eng", "Eng"), 3),   team   = paste0("T", 1:12),   n      = c(6, 3, 8, 2, 5, 4, 3, 7, 2, 6, 4, 5) ) ggplot() +   geom_sketch_sunburst(gss, levels = c("region", "dept", "team"), value = "n",                        fill_by = "root", label = TRUE, label_size = 2.6,                        seed = 7L) +   scale_fill_sketch() +   coord_equal() +   labs(title = "Headcount by region › dept › team") +   theme_void() +   theme(legend.position = "none", plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))

title: Gallery — every geom
text: animate_sketch() animates sketch plot two ways. type = "boil" re-renders shifting every roughening seed per frame, whole drawing shimmers like hand-animated cel; type = "draw_on" reveals finished drawing behind moving wipe, hand drawing . Frames stitched GIF gifski magick installed; otherwise frame paths returned. animates sketch plot change code.  plot drawn , left right:
code: p <- ggplot(mpg, aes(class)) +   geom_sketch_bar(fill = "#7BAFD4", seed = 1L) +   labs(title = "Boiling bars", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch() +   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))  gif <- animate_sketch(p, nframes = 10, fps = 8,                       file = knitr::fig_path(".gif"),                       width = 6, height = 4, res = 110) knitr::include_graphics(gif) drawn <- animate_sketch(p, type = "draw_on", nframes = 14, fps = 10,                         file = knitr::fig_path(".gif"),                         width = 6, height = 4, res = 110) knitr::include_graphics(drawn)

title: Gallery — every geom
text: medium controls stroke laid . sketch_media() lists — pen pencil brush, charcoal, chalk, marker, highlighter, spray; set one constant line/path/segment geom, map aesthetic scale_medium_discrete().
code: sketch_media() #>  [1] "pen"          "ink"          "fountain_pen" "ballpoint"    "brush"        #>  [6] "pencil"       "charcoal"     "pastel"       "chalk"        "marker"       #> [11] "highlighter"  "crayon"       "spray" lv <- c("pencil", "ink", "brush", "charcoal", "marker") waves <- data.frame(x = rep(1:40, length(lv)),                     g = factor(rep(lv, each = 40), levels = lv)) waves$y <- as.integer(waves$g) + sin(waves$x / 4) * 0.3 ggplot(waves, aes(x, y, group = g, medium = g, colour = g)) +   geom_sketch_line(linewidth = 1.1, seed = 1L) +   scale_medium_discrete(media = lv) +   scale_colour_sketch() +   labs(title = "medium mapped to a variable", x = NULL, y = NULL) +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: fill_style = "watercolor" paints stacked translucent washes soft bleeds granulation, polygon-, ribbon-, ellipse-, band-filled geom.
code: ang  <- seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = 11)[-11] r    <- rep(c(1, 0.45), length.out = 10) star <- data.frame(x = r * cos(ang), y = r * sin(ang)) ggplot(star, aes(x, y)) +   geom_sketch_polygon(fill = "#2E86C1", fill_style = "watercolor", seed = 1L) +   coord_equal() +   labs(title = "fill_style = \"watercolor\"") +   theme_sketch() ggplot(mpg, aes(class, hwy, fill = class)) +   geom_sketch_violin(fill_style = "watercolor", seed = 1L, show.legend = FALSE) +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +   labs(title = "Watercolour violins", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch() +   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))

title: Gallery — every geom
text: theme_sketch(paper = ) draws panel textured ground — sketch_papers() lists (notebook, graph, dotted, aged, blueprint, chalkboard, kraft). Dark grounds flip text light automatically.   can also drop paper onto theme via element_sketch_paper():
code: sketch_papers() #> [1] "none"       "notebook"   "graph"      "dotted"     "aged"       #> [6] "blueprint"  "chalkboard" "kraft" ggplot(sales, aes(product, units)) +   geom_sketch_col(fill = "#7BAFD4", seed = 1L) +   labs(title = "Notebook paper", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch(paper = "notebook") ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_sketch_point(colour = "white", seed = 1L) +   labs(title = "Blueprint ground") +   theme_sketch(paper = "blueprint") ggplot(faithful, aes(eruptions, waiting)) +   geom_sketch_point(colour = "#1F618D", seed = 1L) +   theme_sketch() +   theme(panel.background = element_sketch_paper("graph"))

title: Gallery — every geom
text: coord_sketch() roughens gridlines ticks theme — need sketch theme :  coord_sketch_polar() polar companion — wobbly circular grid rose pie-style charts:
code: ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_sketch_point(seed = 1L) +   labs(title = "coord_sketch() under a plain theme") +   coord_sketch(seed = 1L) rose <- data.frame(g = c("a", "b", "c", "d", "e", "f"),                    v = c(3, 5, 2, 4, 6, 3)) ggplot(rose, aes(g, v, fill = g)) +   geom_sketch_col(seed = 1L, show.legend = FALSE) +   scale_fill_sketch() +   coord_sketch_polar(seed = 1L) +   labs(title = "coord_sketch_polar()", x = NULL, y = NULL) +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: Sketch geoms respect full grammar.
code: ggplot(mpg, aes(displ, hwy)) +   geom_sketch_point(size = 2.5, colour = "#34495E", seed = 9L) +   geom_sketch_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, seed = 10L) +   facet_wrap(~drv, labeller = label_both) +   labs(title = "Faceted by drivetrain") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: Every example works theme_sketch(dark = TRUE):
code: ggplot(sales, aes(product, units, fill = product)) +   geom_sketch_col(seed = 1L, show.legend = FALSE) +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +   labs(title = "Dark preset", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch(dark = TRUE)

title: Gallery — every geom
text: default theme_sketch() keeps gridlines, panel border, axis ticks crisp. Pass rough_frame = TRUE frame roughened , matches marks.  roughened elements real theme elements — element_sketch_line() element_sketch_rect() — can also drop theme tune roughness, bowing, seed:
code: ggplot(sales, aes(product, units)) +   geom_sketch_col(fill = "#7BAFD4", seed = 1L) +   labs(title = "Everything wobbles", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch(rough_frame = TRUE, seed = 1L) ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_sketch_point(seed = 1L) +   theme_sketch() +   theme(     panel.grid.major = element_sketch_line(roughness = 0.8, seed = 7L),     axis.ticks       = element_sketch_line(roughness = 0.6, seed = 8L)   )

title: Gallery — every geom
text: scale_colour_sketch() / scale_fill_sketch() use qualitative palette (sketch_palette()) chosen suit hand-drawn look:  continuous data *_sketch_c() variants give ink--paper gradient:
code: ggplot(mpg, aes(displ, hwy, colour = drv)) +   geom_sketch_point(size = 2.5, seed = 1L) +   scale_colour_sketch() +   labs(title = "scale_colour_sketch()") +   theme_sketch(rough_frame = TRUE, seed = 2L) ggplot(faithful, aes(eruptions, waiting, colour = waiting)) +   geom_sketch_point(size = 2.5, seed = 1L) +   scale_colour_sketch_c() +   labs(title = "scale_colour_sketch_c()") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: "scribble" one continuous winding stroke overshoots boundary, like scribbling fill shape:  works anywhere fill_style accepted. ten stroked styles (eleventh, "watercolor", painted rather stroked — see ):
code: ggplot(sales, aes(product, units, fill = product)) +   geom_sketch_col(fill_style = "scribble", seed = 3L, show.legend = FALSE) +   scale_fill_sketch() +   labs(title = "fill_style = \"scribble\"", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch() styles <- c("hachure", "cross_hatch", "zigzag", "zigzag_line", "scribble",             "dots", "dashed", "stipple", "pencil_shade", "solid") grid <- expand.grid(col = 1:5, row = c(2.2, 1)) grid$style <- styles ggplot(grid) +   lapply(seq_len(nrow(grid)), function(i) {     geom_sketch_rect(       data = grid[i, ],       aes(xmin = col - 0.45, xmax = col + 0.45,           ymin = row - 0.35, ymax = row + 0.35),       fill = "#7BAFD4", fill_style = grid$style[i], seed = i     )   }) +   geom_sketch_text(aes(col, row - 0.52, label = style), size = 3.4) +   coord_equal() +   labs(title = "The stroked fill styles", x = NULL, y = NULL) +   theme_sketch() +   theme(axis.text = element_blank())

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_text() picks handwriting face preinstalled OS, results reproduce machine CI runner, register font file explicitly register_sketch_font() font-aware device (ragg, svglite, cairo):
code: register_sketch_font("Caveat", "path/to/Caveat-Regular.ttf")  ggplot(lab, aes(x, y, label = txt)) +   geom_sketch_text(family = "Caveat", size = 8) +   theme_sketch()

title: Getting started with ggsketch
text: geom_sketch_col() draws bars roughened outline hachure (pencil-shading) fill:
code: df <- data.frame(product = c("Alpha", "Bravo", "Charlie", "Delta"),                  units   = c(34, 51, 22, 47))  ggplot(df, aes(product, units)) +   geom_sketch_col(fill = "#7BAFD4", seed = 1L) +   labs(title = "Units sold") +   theme_sketch()

title: Getting started with ggsketch
text: sketch look random, seeded — given seed always produces drawing, figures reproducible. Change seed fresh “hand”.   Set session-wide default options(ggsketch.seed = 1L).
code: base <- ggplot(df, aes(product, units)) + theme_sketch() base + geom_sketch_col(seed = 1L) + labs(subtitle = "seed = 1") base + geom_sketch_col(seed = 7L) + labs(subtitle = "seed = 7")

title: Getting started with ggsketch
text: roughness controls far points displaced (0 = ruler-straight):
code: x <- seq(0, 10, length.out = 40) d <- data.frame(x = x, y = sin(x))  ggplot(d, aes(x, y)) +   geom_sketch_line(aes(colour = "0.0"), roughness = 0,   seed = 2) +   geom_sketch_line(aes(colour = "1.5"), roughness = 1.5, seed = 2) +   geom_sketch_line(aes(colour = "4.0"), roughness = 4,   seed = 2) +   scale_colour_brewer("roughness", palette = "Set1") +   theme_sketch()

title: Getting started with ggsketch
text: Filled geoms (geom_sketch_col(), geom_sketch_rect(), geom_sketch_polygon(), …) take fill_style: fill_style layer parameter, show several draw one layer (panel) per style. cross_hatch:  available styles "hachure", "cross_hatch", "zigzag", "zigzag_line", "scribble", "dots", "dashed", "solid" — plus painted "watercolor" wash.
code: bars <- data.frame(x = c("A", "B", "C"), y = c(4, 6, 3)) ggplot(bars, aes(x, y)) +   geom_sketch_col(fill = "#E8A87C", fill_style = "cross_hatch", seed = 4) +   labs(title = "cross_hatch") +   theme_sketch()

title: Getting started with ggsketch
text: Sketch geoms respect facets, scales, coords:
code: ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_sketch_point(size = 2.5, colour = "#34495E", seed = 9) +   geom_sketch_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, seed = 10) +   facet_wrap(~am, labeller = label_both) +   theme_sketch()

title: Getting started with ggsketch
code: ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_sketch_point(colour = "#E8E6DF", seed = 1L) +   annotate_sketch("rect", xmin = 3, xmax = 4, ymin = 15, ymax = 22,                   fill = NA, colour = "#FFD166", seed = 2L) +   theme_sketch(dark = TRUE)

title: Getting started with ggsketch
text: ggsketch covers far bars lines: distribution geoms (violin, ridgeline, beeswarm, boxplot), part--whole charts (pie, waffle, treemap), streamgraphs, calendar heatmaps, engraving / tonal-shading family, content-aware annotation toolkit (arrows, callouts, brackets, hull marks). drawing look extends — pencil / ink / brush / charcoal media, watercolour fills, textured paper grounds, hand-drawn coords. gallery shows every geom; articles cover fill styles, theming, shared sketch controls depth.

title: Getting started with ggsketch
text: ggsketch built three layers: pure geometry (numbers → numbers), grid grobs roughen device-inch space inside makeContent(), ggproto geoms. algorithms reimplemented original R published descriptions rough.js algorithms hachure approach Wood et al.; rough.js source included. See inst/NOTICE.

title: How it works
text: core-* functions map numbers numbers. take coordinates sketch parameters return roughened coordinates — grid, ggplot2, drawing. rough.js algorithms reimplemented original R: point roughening, cubic-Bézier sampling, ellipse generation, Active-Edge-Table scan-line filler hachure (including concave polygons). ggsketch 2.0 adds pure-geometry modules spirit — variable-width stroke ribbons (stroke_ribbon()), watercolour washes (watercolor_wash()), paper textures (paper_primitives()), squarified treemap layout (treemap_layout()). can call directly: Layer 1 pure, package’s primary regression tests deterministic geometry snapshots — compare numbers exactly, far stable comparing rendered images.
code: # Two passes of a roughened polyline through three points passes <- roughen_polyline(x = c(0, 1, 2), y = c(0, 1, 0),                            roughness = 1, n_passes = 2, seed = 1L) length(passes)       # one matrix per pass #> [1] 2 head(passes[[1]])    # x/y coordinates of the first stroke #>                x           y #> [1,] -0.01208118 0.002597418 #> [2,]  0.10504600 0.093487679 #> [3,]  0.21350587 0.181953799 #> [4,]  0.31377351 0.267744807 #> [5,]  0.40632397 0.350609732 #> [6,]  0.49163232 0.430297605 # Hachure fill of a unit square: a list of line segments segs <- hachure_fill(c(0, 1, 1, 0), c(0, 0, 1, 1),                      hachure_gap = 0.2, hachure_angle = 45, seed = 1L) length(segs) #> [1] 6

title: How it works
text: *_grob() constructors wrap Layer 1 grid grobs whose makeContent() method drawing. Crucially, roughening happens converting absolute device inches: Working inch space (data npc space) means wobble consistent physical scale regardless panel’s aspect ratio output device — makeContent() re-runs draw, resizing plot re-roughens cleanly new size instead stretching cached path.
code: g <- sketch_path_grob(x = c(0.1, 0.5, 0.9), y = c(0.2, 0.8, 0.5),                       roughness = 1, seed = 1L) class(g) #> [1] "SketchPathGrob" "gTree"          "grob"           "gDesc"

title: How it works
text: geom_sketch_*() functions ordinary ggplot2 layers built public extension API (ggproto, draw_panel/draw_group, coord$transform, draw_key). compose everything grammar:
code: ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, colour = factor(cyl))) +   geom_sketch_point(seed = 1L) +   geom_sketch_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, se = FALSE, seed = 2L) +   facet_wrap(~am) +   theme_sketch()

title: How it works
text: Every randomized routine draws seeded local RNG stream restores global random state afterwards. sketch plots reproducible given seed, never disturb set.seed() elsewhere code:
code: set.seed(99) before <- runif(1)  # draw something sketchy (consumes randomness internally) invisible(roughen_polyline(c(0, 1), c(0, 1), seed = 1L))  set.seed(99) after <- runif(1) identical(before, after)   # TRUE — global RNG untouched #> [1] TRUE

title: How it works
text: algorithms reimplemented original R published descriptions rough.js algorithms (Preet Shihn, 2020) hachure approach Wood et al. rough.js source vendored, copied, translated, JavaScript ships package. ggsketch independent R package reimplementing hand-drawn sketch aesthetic first principles. affiliated , derived , endorsed rough.js project, ggrough, related JavaScript libraries. rough.js © Preet Shihn licensed MIT. ggsketch licensed MIT. See inst/NOTICE full attribution.

title: Theming & fonts
text: dark background, give geoms light outline colour (e.g. "grey85") rough strokes read clearly.
code: sales <- data.frame(product = c("Alpha", "Bravo", "Charlie", "Delta"),                     units   = c(34, 51, 22, 47))  ggplot(sales, aes(product, units, fill = product)) +   geom_sketch_col(seed = 1L, show.legend = FALSE) +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +   labs(title = "Light (paper) preset", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch() ggplot(sales, aes(product, units, fill = product)) +   geom_sketch_col(colour = "grey85", seed = 1L, show.legend = FALSE) +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +   labs(title = "Dark (chalkboard) preset", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch(dark = TRUE)

title: Theming & fonts
text: scale_colour_sketch() / scale_fill_sketch() apply muted qualitative palette (sketch_palette()) tuned hand-drawn look; *_sketch_c() variants give continuous ink--paper gradient.
code: ggplot(mpg, aes(displ, hwy, colour = drv)) +   geom_sketch_point(size = 2.5, seed = 1L) +   scale_colour_sketch() +   labs(title = "scale_colour_sketch()") +   theme_sketch() ggplot(faithful, aes(eruptions, waiting, colour = waiting)) +   geom_sketch_point(size = 2.5, seed = 1L) +   scale_colour_sketch_c() +   labs(title = "scale_colour_sketch_c()") +   theme_sketch()

title: Theming & fonts
text: theme_sketch(paper = ) draws panel textured ground. sketch_papers() lists ; dark grounds (blueprint, chalkboard) flip text grid light ink automatically.   finer control, drop paper onto single theme element element_sketch_paper():
code: sketch_papers() #> [1] "none"       "notebook"   "graph"      "dotted"     "aged"       #> [6] "blueprint"  "chalkboard" "kraft" ggplot(sales, aes(product, units, fill = product)) +   geom_sketch_col(seed = 1L, show.legend = FALSE) +   scale_fill_sketch() +   labs(title = "paper = \"notebook\"", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch(paper = "notebook") ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_sketch_point(colour = "white", seed = 1L) +   labs(title = "paper = \"blueprint\"") +   theme_sketch(paper = "blueprint") ggplot(faithful, aes(eruptions, waiting)) +   geom_sketch_point(colour = "#1F618D", seed = 1L) +   theme_sketch() +   theme(panel.background = element_sketch_paper("graph"))

title: Theming & fonts
text: default gridlines, panel border, ticks stay crisp. rough_frame = TRUE roughens match marks:  real theme elements — element_sketch_line() element_sketch_rect() — can roughen individual elements tune roughness, bowing, seed:  coord_sketch() roughens frame theme — even non-sketch one — coord_sketch_polar() circular plots:
code: ggplot(sales, aes(product, units)) +   geom_sketch_col(fill = "#7BAFD4", seed = 1L) +   labs(title = "rough_frame = TRUE", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch(rough_frame = TRUE, seed = 1L) ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_sketch_point(seed = 1L) +   theme_sketch() +   theme(     panel.grid.major = element_sketch_line(roughness = 0.8, seed = 7L),     axis.ticks       = element_sketch_line(roughness = 0.6, seed = 8L)   ) ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_sketch_point(seed = 1L) +   labs(title = "coord_sketch() under theme_bw()") +   coord_sketch(seed = 1L) +   theme_bw() rose <- data.frame(g = c("a", "b", "c", "d", "e", "f"), v = c(3, 5, 2, 4, 6, 3)) ggplot(rose, aes(g, v, fill = g)) +   geom_sketch_col(seed = 1L, show.legend = FALSE) +   scale_fill_sketch() +   coord_sketch_polar(seed = 1L) +   labs(title = "coord_sketch_polar()", x = NULL, y = NULL) +   theme_sketch()

title: Theming & fonts
text: Pass base_family = "auto" style whole theme first installed handwriting font; geom_sketch_text() picks way. Check available machine:
code: ggplot(sales, aes(product, units)) +   geom_sketch_col(fill = "#7BAFD4", seed = 1L) +   geom_sketch_text(aes(label = units), nudge_y = 2.5, size = 6) +   labs(title = "base_family = \"auto\"", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch(base_family = "auto") ggsketch_check_fonts() #> Available handwriting fonts: #>   Caveat

title: Theming & fonts
text: get face machine CI runner — without relying system install — download font (e.g. Caveat Google Fonts) register register_sketch_font(), render font-aware device (ragg, svglite, cairo): ggsketch_check_fonts() register_sketch_font() need optional systemfonts package; without (without handwriting font), everything still renders device default — ggsketch never makes fonts hard dependency.
code: register_sketch_font("Caveat", "~/fonts/Caveat-Regular.ttf")  ggplot(sales, aes(product, units)) +   geom_sketch_col(fill = "#7BAFD4", seed = 1L) +   geom_sketch_text(aes(label = units), family = "Caveat", nudge_y = 2.5, size = 6) +   labs(title = "Registered font", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch(base_family = "Caveat")

title: ggsketch — hand-drawn geoms for ggplot2
text: Grammar-native, hand-drawn geoms ggplot2 - rough.js sketch aesthetic pure R, JavaScript browser.   ggsketch gives ggplot2 geoms (geom_sketch_col(), geom_sketch_line(), geom_sketch_point(), …) render wobbly, hand-drawn look: roughened double-stroke outlines hachure / cross-hatch / zigzag / dots / dashed fills. even shades tonal engraving — continuous tone built hatch-line density, way etcher banknote engraver works (geom_sketch_engrave()), something fill-pattern packages can’t tile motif rather compute tone. geoms real grid grobs wrapped ggproto, compose aes(), stats, scales, facets, coords, draw correctly every R graphics device — screen, PNG, PDF, SVG.

title: ggsketch — hand-drawn geoms for ggplot2
text: ggrough maintainer notes “nice way create sketchy visualisations neat addition {ggplot2} ecosystem.” ggsketch fills gap native geoms.

title: ggsketch — hand-drawn geoms for ggplot2
text: ggsketch pure R (NeedsCompilation: ); hard dependencies ggplot2, grid, rlang, scales, cli, withr. Full documentation gallery every geom: https://orijitghosh.github.io/ggsketch/.
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("orijitghosh/ggsketch")

title: ggsketch — hand-drawn geoms for ggplot2
text: Every randomized routine seeded, given seed always produces wobble — plots reproducible. Set session-wide default options(ggsketch.seed = 1L).
code: library(ggplot2) library(ggsketch)  df <- data.frame(product = c("Alpha","Bravo","Charlie","Delta"),                  units   = c(34, 51, 22, 47))  ggplot(df, aes(product, units)) +   geom_sketch_col(fill = "#7BAFD4", seed = 1L) +   labs(title = "Units sold") +   theme_sketch()

title: ggsketch — hand-drawn geoms for ggplot2
text: Discrete fills map straight aes(fill = …), geom accepts stat - roughened columns coloured Brewer palette, Old Faithful histogram:   Points, marginal rug, hand-drawn linear fit compose like layers; geom_sketch_bracket() adds significance brackets pairwise comparisons:   Every fill style works every filled geom - fill_style = "hachure" boxplots across vehicle classes:   2.0 line adds whole chart families compose + — hierarchy hand-drawn sunburst, weighted flows chord diagram:   also simulates medium, just line. "watercolor" fill paints translucent washes bleed wet--wet regions overlap — mingling new colours — feather along paper tooth set theme_sketch(paper = ):

title: ggsketch — hand-drawn geoms for ggplot2
text: Three layers, kept strictly separate: Layer 1 — pure geometry (R/core-*.R): numbers → numbers. Seeded roughening, ellipse/Bézier sampling, Active-Edge-Table scan-line hachure filler handles concave polygons. grid, ggplot2. Layer 2 — grid grobs (R/grob-*.R): makeContent() converts device inches re-roughens real render size, resizing re-draws cleanly. Layer 3 — ggproto geoms (R/geom-*.R): standard ggplot2 extension API. Roughening always happens inch space, look consistent across aspect ratios devices.

title: ggsketch — hand-drawn geoms for ggplot2
text: algorithms reimplemented original R published descriptions rough.js algorithms (Preet Shihn, 2020) hachure approach Wood et al. rough.js source vendored, copied, translated, JavaScript ships package. See inst/NOTICE. ggsketch independent R package reimplementing hand-drawn sketch aesthetic first principles. affiliated , derived , endorsed rough.js project, ggrough, related JavaScript libraries. rough.js © Preet Shihn licensed MIT. ggsketch licensed MIT.

title: Animate a sketch plot by
text: Renders ggplot built ggsketch geoms nframes times, shifting every roughening seed per-frame amount, whole drawing shimmers re-draws like hand-animated cel ("boiling line" effect). shift added every resolved seed – whether layer set seed inherited global one – plot boils without change code. Frame 1 reproduces static plot exactly.

title: Animate a sketch plot by
code: animate_sketch(   plot,   nframes = 12L,   fps = 10,   type = c("boil", "draw_on"),   direction = c("lr", "rl", "bt", "tb", "diag", "radial"),   easing = c("linear", "ease_in", "ease_out", "ease_in_out"),   file = NULL,   width = 7,   height = 5,   units = "in",   res = 120,   background = "white",   device = NULL,   seed = NULL,   renderer = c("auto", "gifski", "magick", "none"),   loop = TRUE )

title: Animate a sketch plot by
text: plot ggplot object using ggsketch geoms. nframes Number frames. Default 12. fps Frames per second output GIF. Default 10. type Motion type: "boil" (re-seed wobble frame whole drawing shimmers) "draw_on" (progressively reveal finished drawing behind moving wipe, hand drawing ). direction type = "draw_on", drawing revealed: straight wipe "lr" (left--right, default), "rl", "bt" (bottom--top) "tb"; diagonal wipe "diag" (top-left bottom-right); "radial" (circular iris opening centre). easing type = "draw_on", pacing reveal across frames: "linear" (constant speed, default), "ease_in" (start slow), "ease_out" (end slow), "ease_in_out" (slow ends). file Output GIF path. NULL (default), GIF written frame paths returned invisibly. width, height, units, res Frame size resolution, passed graphics device (units one "", "cm", "mm", "px"). background Frame background colour. Default "white". device Graphics device: "ragg" (default installed) "png". seed Base seed (NULL uses getOption("ggsketch.seed", 1L)). renderer GIF backend: "auto" (default), "gifski", "magick", "none" (always return frame paths). loop Loop GIF forever? Default TRUE.

title: Animate a sketch plot by
text: Invisibly, GIF path (written) character vector frame image paths.

title: Animate a sketch plot by
text: Frames stitched GIF gifski magick installed (neither hard dependency); otherwise frame image paths returned can assemble . Everything reproducible: seed yields animation.

title: Animate a sketch plot by
code: # \donttest{ library(ggplot2) p <- ggplot(mpg, aes(class)) +   geom_sketch_bar(fill = "#7BAFD4", seed = 1L) +   theme_sketch() # Write a GIF if gifski/magick is available, else get frame paths back: gif <- animate_sketch(p, nframes = 8, file = tempfile(fileext = ".gif")) # }

title: Sketchy annotations — annotate_sketch

title: Sketchy annotations — annotate_sketch
code: annotate_sketch(   geom,   x = NULL,   y = NULL,   xmin = NULL,   xmax = NULL,   ymin = NULL,   ymax = NULL,   xend = NULL,   yend = NULL,   r = NULL,   a = NULL,   b = NULL,   seed = NULL,   na.rm = FALSE,   ... )

title: Sketchy annotations — annotate_sketch
text: geom Name sketch geom draw. One "point", "line", "path", "segment", "rect", "polygon", "circle", "ellipse". x, y, xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax, xend, yend Positioning aesthetics (numeric vectors, recycled common length). Supply ones chosen geom needs (e.g. xmin/xmax/ymin/ymax "rect", xend/yend "segment"). r, , b Radius ("circle") semi-axes ("ellipse"). seed Integer seed reproducible wobble. na.rm FALSE (default), missing values trigger warning. ... arguments passed underlying geom_sketch_*() layer (e.g. colour, fill, roughness, fill_style).

text: x, y, xend, yend Endpoint positions data units (vectors recycle). annotate_sketch_underline() horizontal: yend defaults y. colour Ink colour. Highlight defaults fluorescent yellow. linewidth Stroke width. highlighter medium multiplies wide band; underline stays line. seed Integer seed reproducible wobble. ... arguments passed geom_sketch_segment(). strokes Number overlapped underline strokes. Default 1. roughness Wobble amount. Underlines default little shakier. bowing Bow underline stroke (kept low repeated strokes stay together). Default 0.4.

title: Highlighter swipes and hand-drawn underlines — annotate_sketch_highlight
text: annotate_sketch_highlight() lays wide, translucent chisel-tip band ("highlighter" medium) (x, y) (xend, yend) – swipe line, label region interest like fluorescent marker. annotate_sketch_underline() draws quick wobbly stroke (x, y) (xend, y) – underline data point piece text. strokes > 1 re-draws fresh wobble emphatic scrawl.

title: Highlighter swipes and hand-drawn underlines — annotate_sketch_highlight

what: Boil a gganimate animation (sketch wobble + data transitions) — boil_gganimate
title: Boil a gganimate animation (sketch wobble + data transitions) — boil_gganimate
text: Render gganimate animation - plot built ggsketch geoms plus transition_*() - , top gganimate's data tweening, hand-drawn lines boil: every roughening seed shifted per frame, drawing shimmers re-draws like hand-animated cel bars grow, points fly, line draws along x. moving-data companion animate_sketch(), boils static plot.

title: Boil a gganimate animation (sketch wobble + data transitions) — boil_gganimate
code: boil_gganimate(   animation,   nframes = 100L,   fps = 10,   intensity = 1,   detail = 1L,   file = NULL,   width = 7,   height = 5,   units = "in",   res = 120,   background = "white",   device = NULL,   seed = NULL,   renderer = c("auto", "gifski", "magick", "none"),   loop = TRUE )

title: Boil a gganimate animation (sketch wobble + data transitions) — boil_gganimate
text: animation gganimate animation: ggplot using ggsketch geoms transition_*() added (class gganim). nframes Number frames. Default 100 (gganimate's default). fps Frames per second output. Default 10. intensity Boil strength: scales far seed jitter steps frame. 1 (default) matches animate_sketch(); higher shimmers . detail Tween sub-frames per frame, passed gganimate's prerender smoother motion (rendered frames sampled back nframes). Default 1. file Output path (e.g. .gif). NULL (default), file written frame paths returned invisibly. width, height, units, res Frame size resolution. units one "", "cm", "mm", "px". background Frame background colour. Default "white". device Graphics device: NULL (default; "ragg" installed, else "png"), "ragg"/"png", device string gganimate accepts ("ragg_png", "svglite", ...). seed Integer offset boil sequence (NULL = 0, frame 1 un-boiled). Change vary shimmer without touching plot. renderer GIF backend: "auto" (default), "gifski", "magick", "none" (always return frame paths). loop Loop output forever? Default TRUE.

title: Boil a gganimate animation (sketch wobble + data transitions) — boil_gganimate
text: Invisibly, output path (written) character vector frame image paths.

title: Boil a gganimate animation (sketch wobble + data transitions) — boil_gganimate
text: boil rides global ggsketch.seed_jitter option (lever animate_sketch() uses), advanced per-frame counter frame 1 un-boiled drawing whole animation reproducible. Frames stitched backend animate_sketch() (gifski magick); neither installed frame paths returned. gganimate optional dependency.

title: Boil a gganimate animation (sketch wobble + data transitions) — boil_gganimate
code: # \donttest{ library(ggplot2) library(gganimate) p <- ggplot(mpg, aes(class, fill = drv)) +   geom_sketch_bar(position = "dodge", seed = 1L) +   scale_fill_sketch() +   theme_sketch() +   transition_states(drv, transition_length = 2, state_length = 1) boil_gganimate(p, nframes = 30, file = tempfile(fileext = ".gif")) # }
